helpful: Provisioned petition.txt and questions-presented.txt were the decisive inputs — full QP and Reasons-for-Granting text made the vehicle analysis possible without any fetching.
Per-Term fee-class detail in statpack.json (paid vs IFP grant rates) was the single most useful quantitative anchor for a paid petition.
gaps: No topic/subject filter on SCOTUS corpus rows, so `fedcourts query` cannot surface issue-similar cert priors (e.g., other First Amendment employee-speech petitions); a QP-text similarity search would help.
The statpack's salience-band table described in the predict prompt is not present in the committed statpack.md/json for this run.
A pre-conference signal cut conditioned on 'respondent waived + no CFR yet' would be a sharper anchor than the realized relist-count table, which is unknowable pre-conference.
notes: Unpublished Sixth Circuit opinions are absent from CourtListener's opinion index; the petition's own account of the decision below was the only source for the lower-court holding.
